goal: Re-land the knowledge_graph CRUD + bi-temporal queries + hot-staging writes onto main and flip P1-W4-F02 and P1-W4-F08 to passes=true. Supersedes WO-0012, WO-0020, and WO-0024. The sole blocker that rejected WO-0024 (pre-existing rustdoc ambiguity in crates/ucil-core/src/incremental.rs) is now resolved on main at commit f6ec86e, so a clean re-verification is expected.
plan_summary: The implementation is already present on the stale feat/WO-0024-kg-crud-and-hot-staging branch across 9 cleanly-scoped commits (d13cfc9..60eab33). The executor's simplest path is: (a) create feat/WO-0030-kg-crud-hot-staging-retry from current main (which includes the rustdoc fix f6ec86e); (b) cherry-pick the 9 implementation commits in order; (c) confirm `cargo doc -p ucil-core --no-deps` now exits green on this new branch; (d) write the ready-for-review marker. The executor MAY re-implement from scratch if cherry-picking encounters a conflict, in which case the scope_in below is the full specification — it mirrors WO-0024's scope_in verbatim with only the branch-name changes. This unblocks the Week-4 cascade: P1-W4-F03 (symbol resolution), P1-W4-F04 (extraction pipeline), P1-W4-F10 (get_conventions), and transitively P1-W4-F05, P1-W4-F09, P1-W5-F02, P1-W5-F09.
scope_in: PREFERRED PATH — cherry-pick reuse: `git checkout -b feat/WO-0030-kg-crud-hot-staging-retry origin/main && git cherry-pick d13cfc9..60eab33` (9 commits from feat/WO-0024-kg-crud-and-hot-staging). Then run `cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo nextest run -p ucil-core knowledge_graph:: && cargo doc -p ucil-core --no-deps` locally — all must be green. Push and write the WO-0030 ready-for-review marker.

FALLBACK PATH — re-implement: only if cherry-pick hits an unresolvable conflict with current main (WO-0024 was based on main tip cc7e1ba; current main is 373e1ee — intervening merges WO-0026, WO-0028, WO-0029 touched unrelated crates/ucil-daemon + crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics files but NOT crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs nor Cargo.toml workspace deps, so conflicts are unlikely). The fallback scope is the full WO-0024 `scope_in` contract, which is reproduced in condensed form across the following bullets:

Cargo.toml (workspace root) — add `chrono = { version = "0.4", default-features = false, features = ["clock", "serde", "std"] }` to `[workspace.dependencies]` (default-features OFF avoids the `oldtime` / `time 0.1` transitive chain)
crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml — add `chrono = { workspace = true }` under `[dependencies]`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — add `pub struct Entity` with fields (exact order): `pub id: Option<i64>`, `pub kind: String`, `pub name: String`, `pub qualified_name: Option<String>`, `pub file_path: String`, `pub start_line: Option<i64>`, `pub end_line: Option<i64>`, `pub signature: Option<String>`, `pub doc_comment: Option<String>`, `pub language: Option<String>`, `pub t_valid_from: Option<String>` (RFC-3339), `pub t_valid_to: Option<String>` (RFC-3339), `pub importance: f64`, `pub source_tool: Option<String>`, `pub source_hash: Option<String>` — derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — add `pub struct Relation` with fields: `pub id: Option<i64>`, `pub source_id: i64`, `pub target_id: i64`, `pub kind: String`, `pub weight: f64`, `pub t_valid_from: Option<String>`, `pub t_valid_to: Option<String>`, `pub source_tool: Option<String>`, `pub source_evidence: Option<String>`, `pub confidence: f64` — derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — add `pub struct HotObservation` with fields: `pub raw_text: String`, `pub session_id: Option<String>`, `pub related_file: Option<String>`, `pub related_symbol: Option<String>` — derives `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `pub enum WalCheckpointMode { Passive, Truncate }` with `impl WalCheckpointMode { const fn as_sql(self) -> &'static str { match self { Self::Passive => "PASSIVE", Self::Truncate => "TRUNCATE" } } }` — derives `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::upsert_entity(&mut self, entity: &Entity) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` — routes through `execute_in_transaction`; INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(qualified_name, file_path, t_valid_from) DO UPDATE SET t_last_verified = datetime('now'), access_count = access_count + 1 RETURNING id. `#[tracing::instrument(level="debug", skip(self, entity), fields(name=%entity.name))]`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::get_entity_by_qualified_name(&self, qualified_name: &str, file_path: Option<&str>) -> Result<Option<Entity>, KnowledgeGraphError>` — read-only; returns the most recent row (ORDER BY t_ingested_at DESC LIMIT 1)
crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::list_entities_by_file(&self, file_path: &str) -> Result<Vec<Entity>, KnowledgeGraphError>` — read-only; ORDER BY start_line ascending

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::get_entity_as_of(&self, qualified_name: &str, at: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>) -> Result<Option<Entity>, KnowledgeGraphError>` — bi-temporal read using `at.to_rfc3339()` for lexicographic comparison. `#[tracing::instrument(level="debug", skip(self))]`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::upsert_relation(&mut self, relation: &Relation) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` — routes through `execute_in_transaction`; INSERT ... RETURNING id (no ON CONFLICT — §12.1 `relations` has no UNIQUE constraint)
crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::list_relations_by_source(&self, source_id: i64) -> Result<Vec<Relation>, KnowledgeGraphError>` — read-only

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::stage_hot_observation(&mut self, observation: &HotObservation) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` — routes through `execute_in_transaction` (BEGIN IMMEDIATE); INSERT ... RETURNING id

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::stage_hot_convention_signal(&mut self, pattern_hash: &str, file_path: &str, example_snippet: Option<&str>) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` — routes through `execute_in_transaction`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::stage_hot_architecture_delta(&mut self, change_type: &str, file_path: &str, details: Option<&str>) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` — routes through `execute_in_transaction`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — `KnowledgeGraph::checkpoint_wal(&self, mode: WalCheckpointMode) -> Result<(i64, i64, i64), KnowledgeGraphError>` — wraps `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(<MODE>)`; returns (busy, log_frames, checkpointed). `#[tracing::instrument(level="debug", skip(self))]`

crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs — extend `KnowledgeGraphError` with `#[error("invalid timestamp: {0}")] InvalidTimestamp(String)` (rusqlite errors continue to flow through the existing `#[from] rusqlite::Error` arm). `#[non_exhaustive]` already on the enum
crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs — extend the existing re-export to emit `pub use knowledge_graph::{Entity, HotObservation, KnowledgeGraph, KnowledgeGraphError, Relation, WalCheckpointMode};`
Module-root tests in `knowledge_graph.rs` — ALL at module level under `#[cfg(test)] #[test]` (DEC-0005 precedent). Required tests (`test_hot_staging_writes` is the F08 FROZEN SELECTOR):
* `test_upsert_and_get_entity` — inserts an Entity, calls `get_entity_by_qualified_name`, asserts all fields round-trip (including Option<String> fields)
* `test_bi_temporal_as_of` — inserts three Entity rows for the same qualified_name with staggered t_valid_from / t_valid_to; queries at two timestamps; asserts the correct row is returned each time
* `test_list_entities_by_file` — inserts 3 Entity rows with ascending start_line; asserts document-order return
* `test_upsert_relation_and_list` — inserts 2 Entity rows, then a Relation; calls `list_relations_by_source`; asserts all fields intact

* `test_entity_unique_constraint_updates` — inserts the same (qualified_name, file_path, t_valid_from) twice; asserts the second call returns the same id, increments access_count, and updates t_last_verified

* `test_hot_staging_writes` — **FROZEN F08 SELECTOR**. Calls stage_hot_observation, stage_hot_convention_signal, stage_hot_architecture_delta once each; asserts each COUNT(*) is 1 and returned ids are positive
* `test_wal_checkpoint_truncates` — writes ≥10 rows to hot_observations; calls `checkpoint_wal(WalCheckpointMode::Truncate)`; asserts (busy, log_frames, checkpointed) tuple returned with busy == 0

Rustdoc on every new public item (Entity, Relation, HotObservation, WalCheckpointMode, each new KnowledgeGraph::* method). Cite master-plan §12.1 (schema), §11 line 1117 (BEGIN IMMEDIATE), §15.2 (tracing spans). `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` is active — use unambiguous link forms. The pre-existing `incremental.rs` rustdoc bug that rejected WO-0024 is already fixed on main at f6ec86e.
scope_out: crates/ucil-core/src/incremental.rs — DO NOT touch; the rustdoc ambiguity fix already landed via WO-0025 (f6ec86e)
Any work on feat/WO-0024-kg-crud-and-hot-staging branch itself — leave it as a historical marker; do not push commits to it
P1-W4-F03 (symbol resolution) — separate downstream WO after F02 lands
P1-W4-F04 (tree-sitter extraction pipeline → KG) — separate downstream WO
Any changes to crates/ucil-daemon/ or crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/ (WO-0026 / WO-0028 / WO-0029 merges into main since WO-0024 was cut — those areas are out of scope here)
Any changes to feature-list.json (only the verifier flips passes via scripts/flip-feature.sh)
Any changes to scripts/gate/** or scripts/flip-feature.sh
Re-running the WO-0025 rustdoc disambiguation — already merged
acceptance: Branch feat/WO-0030-kg-crud-hot-staging-retry exists on origin with the 9 implementation commits (either cherry-picked or re-implemented) plus the ready-for-review marker
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-core knowledge_graph::` — all 7 tests green (P1-W4-F02 frozen selector is the whole module; P1-W4-F08 frozen selector is test_hot_staging_writes)
`cargo doc -p ucil-core --no-deps` — exits 0 with no ^warning/^error lines (was the WO-0024 rejection criterion; now unblocked by f6ec86e)
Each required test exists at the documented name and is a real #[test] (not #[ignore])
All new public items have rustdoc with master-plan citations per .claude/rules/rust-style.md § Docs
